albums.title: Discovery
albums.reason: With 2940 all-time plays, Daft Punk is your most-played artist ever — and Discovery is the record that defined a generation of those listens, from Digital Love to Superheroes.

albums.title: OK Computer
albums.reason: Radiohead sits at 2050 all-time plays and Reckoner alone has 125 spins — OK Computer is the gravitational centre of that love, a record you've probably not revisited in full recently.

albums.title: Thriller
albums.reason: Michael Jackson's 1852 all-time plays and top-50 tracks like Human Nature, Billie Jean and Black or White all trace back to this era — Thriller is the deep vault that made him your third most-played artist.

albums.title: Houses of the Holy
albums.reason: Led Zeppelin sits at 964 all-time plays and Kashmir and The Rain Song are deep in your history — Houses of the Holy is the album between your most-played Zeppelin records that deserves a full revisit.

albums.title: Hot Fuss
albums.reason: Mr. Brightside (68 plays), A Dustland Fairytale and All These Things That I've Done all point to 926 plays of Killers love — Hot Fuss is where it all began and it's been too long.

albums.title: Rhythm and Stealth
albums.reason: Release the Pressure has 120 all-time plays and Leftfield sits at 1016 — Rhythm and Stealth is their second album and a darker, deeper companion to Leftism that you probably haven't touched in years.

albums.title: Assume Form
albums.reason: James Blake has 637 all-time plays with Postpone at 156 spins — Assume Form is his most warmly melodic record and sits squarely in the recent-vault era, distinct from the earlier albums already recommended.

albums.title: Outlier
albums.reason: Bonobo has 440 all-time plays and still features in your last 12 months — Outlier is his most recent studio work, a direct extension of the North Borders and Migration arc you've loved.

albums.title: Sixteen Oceans
albums.reason: Four Tet has been a fixture across your all-time and 12-month listening, and Sixteen Oceans — with its balance of soft beats and emotional melody — fits perfectly between Beautiful Rewind and There Is Love in You in your collection.

albums.title: Memoria
albums.reason: Trentemøller sits at 560 all-time plays and The Last Resort and Lost are already in your history — Memoria is his most recent album, atmospheric and cinematic, and a natural next chapter for you.

albums.title: Love Hope
albums.reason: Maribou State has 434 all-time plays and Beginner's Luck is a top-50 track for you — Love Hope is their latest record and sits in the recent-vault window, building on Portraits and Kingdoms in Colour.

albums.title: MORE D4TA
albums.reason: Moderat's trilogy (I, II, III) is all in your recommended history and Rusty Nails is a top-50 track — MORE D4TA is their long-awaited 2022 return and sits perfectly in the recent-vault slot.

albums.title: Head Over Heels
albums.reason: Your love of Daft Punk, Justice and electronic funk makes Chromeo's Head Over Heels a natural fit — it's their most polished record and somehow never made it into your rotation despite sitting squarely in your lane.

albums.title: A Real Hero
albums.reason: You deeply love Air, Daft Punk and the French electronic sound — College's A Real Hero EP (expanded) is a cult gem of that world, cinematic and emotionally charged, and almost certainly off your radar.

albums.title: You Make Me Feel
albums.reason: Your top tracks include James Blake and deep atmospheric electronic — Milosh's You Make Me Feel predates the whole minimal-electronic-soul wave and is a remarkably beautiful record that sits right in that space.

albums.title: Earwax
albums.reason: Your affection for Orbital, Leftfield and Aphex Twin points toward the darker end of 90s/00s electronic — Headless Horseman's Earwax is a cult classic in that world, hypnotic and relentless, and likely completely new to you.

albums.title: Geometry
albums.reason: Plaid and Aphex Twin are deep in your all-time catalogue — Jega's Geometry is a Warp-adjacent gem from the same golden era of IDM that somehow slipped through the cracks, precise and emotionally resonant.

albums.title: Neon Golden
albums.reason: Your love of Radiohead's atmospheric weight and James Blake's introspection maps perfectly onto The Notwist's Neon Golden — a beloved cult record that blends electronic beats with deeply human songwriting.

albums.title: Bring Backs
albums.reason: Your recent rotation of Portico Quartet, Yussef Dayes and Bill Withers suggests a deep pull toward jazz with soul and groove — Alfa Mist's Bring Backs is warm, effortless and sits exactly in that current arc.

albums.title: Polyhymnia
albums.reason: Portico Quartet and Yussef Dayes are shaping your current listening and Yazz Ahmed's Polyhymnia — jazz trumpet filtered through electronic texture and Middle Eastern influence — is a natural and thrilling next step.

albums.title: Moto Perpetuo
albums.reason: Nils Frahm, Hania Rani and Joep Beving are all active in your 12-month listening — Bror Forsgren's Moto Perpetuo is a quieter, contemplative discovery in that Erased Tapes-adjacent space that you'd likely love immediately.

albums.title: Dreamtime
albums.reason: Your recent listening to Sault, Charles Bradley and Bill Withers shows a clear pull toward deep soul — Tendai's Dreamtime is a gorgeous neo-soul record full of warmth and craft that fits your current mood perfectly.

albums.title: Siku
albums.reason: Khruangbin and Hermanos Gutiérrez dominate your recent listening with their warm, sun-baked feel — Nicola Cruz's Siku adds Andean texture and organic electronics to that same dreamy world-music space.

albums.title: What We Call Life
albums.reason: Your current arc toward Sault, Khruangbin and Bonobo — organic, groove-rooted and soulful — makes Jordan Rakei's What We Call Life a perfect discovery, a deeply personal album with immaculate production.

albums.title: Texas Sun
albums.reason: Khruangbin is your most-played artist this month by a mile and you've been looping May Ninth and Mariella — Texas Sun is the EP bridge between Con Todo El Mundo and Mordechai and fits exactly where your head is right now.

albums.title: Memory Streams
albums.reason: Ruins has been on heavy repeat this week and Portico Quartet is your second most-played artist this month — Memory Streams is a lesser-revisited album in their catalogue that matches the meditative quality you're clearly in.

albums.title: Spells
albums.reason: Golden Times 1 has been in your most recent tracks multiple times this week — Spells (the original, not Special Edition) is the foundation of that love and deserves a full listen in this reflective, quiet mood.

albums.title: Noche Americana
albums.reason: Hermanos Gutiérrez is your third most-played artist this month and you've been through 8 Años and Hijos Del Sol recently — Noche Americana is an earlier album with the same dusty, cinematic guitar warmth you're clearly craving.

albums.title: Black Classical Music
albums.reason: Birds of Paradise was literally your most recent track played this week — Black Classical Music is the full album context for that moment, and with Khruangbin and Portico in your current rotation it fits the mood perfectly.

albums.title: Dial 'M' for Monkey
albums.reason: Bonobo has been in your last month's rotation and you've already circled through Black Sands and Migration via recommendations — Dial 'M' for Monkey is the overlooked early gem, dusty and organic, perfect for this slow, warm moment.

songs.title: Comfortably Numb
songs.reason: Pink Floyd sits at 1199 all-time plays and you've revisited The Wall and Dark Side via recommendations — Comfortably Numb is the one track from that catalogue that earns a standalone deep-vault moment right now.

songs.title: Eple
songs.reason: Röyksopp has 1062 all-time plays and The Girl and the Robot is a top-50 track — Eple is the earlier gem from Melody A.M. that defined that whole tender, glassy Norwegian electronic sound you fell in love with.

songs.title: Unfinished Sympathy
songs.reason: Massive Attack's 534 all-time plays and Teardrop in your recommended history point to deep love for the Blue Lines era — Unfinished Sympathy is arguably their greatest single moment and belongs in a deep-vault rotation.

songs.title: All I Need
songs.reason: Air has 1011 all-time plays, Sexy Boy and La femme d'argent are top-50 tracks — All I Need is the quiet heartbreaker from Moon Safari that rewards a deep, patient listen after years away.

songs.title: Block Rockin' Beats
songs.reason: The Chemical Brothers sit at 556 all-time plays and Let Forever Be and The Sunshine Underground are in your history — Block Rockin' Beats is the defining moment of their peak era and pure deep-vault gold.

songs.title: Pearl's Girl
songs.reason: Underworld has 633 all-time plays with Born Slippy at 88 plays — Pearl's Girl is the overlooked gem from the Second Toughest era, relentless and transcendent, and probably hasn't crossed your ears in a decade.

songs.title: Cirrus
songs.reason: Bonobo is across both your all-time and 12-month lists — Cirrus from The North Borders is the kinetic centrepiece of his mid-career peak and sits perfectly in the recent-vault era.

songs.title: Nervous Tics
songs.reason: Maribou State has 434 all-time plays and Kingdoms in Colour is in your recommended history — Nervous Tics is the standout from that album, patient and emotionally intelligent in a way that suits the recent-vault era.

songs.title: Parallel Jalebi
songs.reason: Four Tet has been present across your all-time and 12-month listening — Parallel Jalebi from New Energy is a quiet revelation, drifting and warm, distinct from the tracks already in your recommended list.

songs.title: I Need a Forest Fire
songs.reason: James Blake's Postpone sits at 156 plays and The Colour in Anything is in your recommended history — I Need a Forest Fire is the towering highlight of that album, with Bon Iver, cinematic and emotionally overwhelming.

songs.title: Goodbye
songs.reason: Apparat has 36 plays in the last 12 months and A Hum of Maybe is your most-played album from them recently — Goodbye from Walls is a landmark track in your history and deserves its recent-vault spotlight.

songs.title: Rykketid
songs.reason: Trentemøller has 560 all-time plays and Into the Trees and Take Me Into Your Skin are in your history — Rykketid from Fixion is a haunting, underplayed gem that fits the recent-vault era without overlapping your existing picks.

songs.title: My Friend Dario
songs.reason: Your love of Daft Punk, Justice and the French electronic scene makes Vitalic an obvious adjacent artist you've somehow missed — My Friend Dario from OK Cowboy is abrasive and euphoric in exactly the right way.

songs.title: Lucky Cat
songs.reason: Plaid and Boards of Canada occupy your all-time list and Isan sits in precisely that Skam/Morr Music world of intricate, bittersweet electronic miniatures — Lucky Cat is one of their most beloved tracks.

songs.title: Fires That None Can Light
songs.reason: Your deep love of Moby, Massive Attack and the quieter end of electronic music makes Manual's hazy, beatific ambient-pop a natural fit — this track is a cult favourite that sits perfectly in your lane.

songs.title: Morning Star
songs.reason: Röyksopp and Air are your heartland artists — Flunk are a Norwegian act working in exactly that space, and Morning Star is a hushed, beautiful track that should have been in your collection long ago.

songs.title: Red Safe World
songs.reason: Massive Attack, Moby and the atmospheric end of your taste all point toward Arms and Sleepers — this track from their Matador album is a slow-building, cinematic gem that lives in the same emotional register as Teardrop.

songs.title: Fragment
songs.reason: Your deep pull toward Aphex Twin, Orbital and atmospheric electronic architecture makes Aes Dana a compelling discovery — Fragment is a signature track from their Leylines album, meditative and perfectly constructed.

songs.title: Baobab
songs.reason: Your current arc through Khruangbin, Hermanos Gutiérrez and St Germain suggests a love of warm, world-influenced groove — Rob Arlett's Baobab sits in that Afro-jazz-meets-electronic space with effortless cool.

songs.title: Contact
songs.reason: DJ Koze and Four Tet are in your 12-month rotation and Hesketh works in a similar late-night, emotionally charged deep house space — Contact is a standout that captures the same warmth you've been gravitating toward.

songs.title: Elusive
songs.reason: Your recent listening to Nightmares on Wax, Kinobe and St Germain maps directly onto Gogo Sho's slow, soulful electronic approach — Elusive is a quietly hypnotic track that fits your current introspective pace.

songs.title: Color Your Life
songs.reason: Bill Withers, Charles Bradley and Syl Johnson are shaping your 12-month soul listening — Sequoyah Murray occupies a similar space of deeply felt, unhurried soul and Color Your Life is an immediate, beautiful entry point.

songs.title: Coffee
songs.reason: Bonobo and Khruangbin dominate your current listening and Catching Flies works in a similar organic, unhurried groove world — Coffee is a gentle, warm track that would slot perfectly into your current rotation.

songs.title: meet me in brooklyn
songs.reason: Your recent listening to Sault, Dusty Springfield and Charles Bradley reveals a love of raw, emotional soul — Yaya Bey's meet me in brooklyn is hushed, intimate and soulful in a way that connects directly to where you are now.

songs.title: Pelota
songs.reason: You've been on a serious Khruangbin loop this week with May Ninth and Mariella on repeat — Pelota from Mordechai is the hypnotic, gently rolling companion track that fits exactly this slow, meditative headspace.

songs.title: Lullaby for Humans
songs.reason: Ruins has been your most-played track across multiple recent sessions — Lullaby for Humans from Art in the Age of Automation is the emotional counterpart, slow and enveloping, perfect for this reflective mood.

songs.title: When I'm Called
songs.reason: Frolic was literally in your most recent tracks this week — When I'm Called from the same album Good and Green Again is the deeper cut that rewards the same quiet, unhurried attention you're clearly in right now.

songs.title: Afro Cubanism
songs.reason: Birds of Paradise opened your most recent listening session and the full Black Classical Music album is this week's discovery — Afro Cubanism feat. Tom Misch is the rhythmically alive peak of that record and your current energy.

songs.title: Pygmalion
songs.reason: Golden Times 1 has appeared three times in your most recent listening — Pygmalion from the same Spells Special Edition is the expansive, slowly unfolding companion piece that matches this reflective, patient mood.

songs.title: El Camino Eternal
songs.reason: Hermanos Gutiérrez is your third most-played artist this month — El Camino Eternal is the slow, sun-bleached centrepiece of their sound, perfect for the warm, drifting mood your recent plays are pointing toward.

djSets.setName: HMWL Podcast 430: Niconé & David Hasert (Kompakt / Get Physical / HMWL)
